evidence.observation: Skill §10 iter-3 closing paragraph reads (verbatim): 'Recommendation for iteration 4: surface the still-flat chucker rate (~15%, target 25–40% per PRD-002 R-29) as the gameplay lever (nail-adjustment 釘調整) rather than a static config — let the player retune the funnel and watch the rate respond. That makes the §9 economy meaningful: better nail layouts yield better ベース which yields more chucker hits per yen spent.' This recommendation was written into the skill at iter 3 close.
evidence.notes: Diagnostic for both H1 (the recommendation predates this audit and is project-level institutional knowledge) AND H3 (both subagents read this when making their independent recommendation). Reading H1 and H3 together: the convergence is real evidence even if anchored, because the anchor itself is a *prior audit's conclusion*, not a fresh prior.

evidence.observation: Two parallel general-purpose subagents (one focused on UI, one on mechanics) independently produced research notes (research/iter4-ui-options.md, research/iter4-mechanics-options.md) and both named nail-adjustment 釘調整 as their recommended Direction. The UI subagent named it 'Direction C: Contextual Minimalism + Diegetic Agency'; the mechanics subagent named it 'Direction A: Nail-adjustment 釘調整 — make ベース the agentic core.' Their reasoning chains overlap but their secondary recommendations diverged (UI: Cinematic Revenge as second-best; mechanics: Session arc as second-best).
evidence.notes: Reliability B because subagent reasoning is opaque (we see their outputs, not their full deliberation). The divergent runner-ups are diagnostic against H3 — if both subagents were merely echoing §10, their runner-ups would also converge.

evidence.observation: Empirical chucker rate measured at ~15% in the iter-3 headless playwright probe (5 balls/sec auto-fire for ~30 seconds, captured in the live-Pages probe screenshots iter3-final.png and similar). PRD-002 R-29 target band is 25–40%. The gap is the only empirically-measured PRD violation currently on the board.
evidence.notes: Diagnostic for H1 (the recommendation closes a measured defect) and H4 (it IS the cherrypickable one — the other §14 tests are not measured at this horizon). Note: 30-second probe is not statistically rigorous; longer probes would tighten the CI. The 25–40% band targets the long-run average.

evidence.observation: Skill §11.2 gambler's-ruin runway formula: typical session runway = B/u where u = unit-loss-per-spin. For ベース at 35% vs 15%, u drops nearly in half (roughly ¥3/spin at 35% vs ¥5–6/spin at 15%). Runway approximately doubles at 35% versus 15%. This directly addresses skill §14 test 4 (2-hour pacing) by making longer sessions sustainable on the same ball budget.
evidence.notes: Math-grounded prediction that closing the ベース gap *also* closes test 4 (session length) — defuses H4's cherrypick concern by showing the measured fix has a chain effect on the unmeasured gaps.

evidence.observation: Audit-001 (the prior architecture audit) H4 (cherrypick) was: 'We treated tested invariants as a proxy for intent satisfied, cherrypicking the measurable subset.' Iter-3's response was to add the visible event animations and economy elements that addressed parts of the unmeasured C-10/C-3 concerns, BUT skill §14 tests 4 and 5 remain unmeasured.
evidence.notes: Pattern-recurrence evidence for H4. The audit-001 cherrypick pattern is still active; iter-4 must explicitly call this out and either address the unmeasured tests or document deferral with rationale.

evidence.observation: Both subagents identified the same 30-second-sniff-test UX risk (UI subagent open Q3, mechanics subagent open Q1): if the tuning UI is visible on first impression, the cabinet reads as 'video-game options menu' rather than 'pachinko machine.' Both proposed the same mitigation: hidden-by-default mirroring the iter-3 data-lamp toggle pattern, plus chapter-gating (locked at chapter 1, unlocked progressively).
evidence.notes: Independent identification of the same risk + same mitigation is evidence AGAINST H3 (mere echoing) — the subagents reasoned forward to the same UX call from different starting points. Diagnostic for H2 (drift): the mitigation IS the discipline that resolves the C-9 drift concern.

evidence.observation: Intent open question 6 (WASM persistence shim — quad-storage vs sapp-jsutils vs other) is still open and has been on the deferred list since iter 1. PRD-001 R-18..R-20 are marked native-only in iter-3's §Platform notes section. Iter-4's nail-adjustment AND welcome-back card both need persistence; the deferred-since-iter-1 question is now on the iter-4 critical path.
evidence.notes: Mild evidence against H1 (the chosen direction has more dependencies than its alternatives). Direction B (session arc) and Direction C (reach engineering) could ship without resolving persistence; Direction A/winner requires it. The audit's key judgment includes this in the remainingGaps.

evidence.observation: Intent open question 7 (BGM crossfade primitive — accept hard-cut as snap-to-keyframe grammar, vs extend quad-snd, vs swap audio crate) is also still open. Iter-3 ships with the hard-cut acknowledged in PRD-003 §Platform notes. Iter-4's contextual-minimalism direction (UI Direction C, which is part of the chosen synthesis) leans on audio crossfade for reach signalling — moving the signal from banner-text to audio-led environmental cues.
evidence.notes: Same pattern as E7. The chosen direction unblocks-or-blocks-on previously-deferred questions. This is not a fatal flaw — but it does increase iter-4's risk profile.

evidence.observation: PRD-002 R-29 reads: 'The chucker hit rate is calibrated so ~25–40 balls returned per 100 fired (PRD-001 §4 base game rate ベース). Tune via pin layout.' The wording 'is calibrated' presupposes a static value set by the designer. Under iter-4 the rate would be player-influenced; R-29's contract becomes ambiguous.
evidence.notes: Strong evidence for H5 (tension): the C-2 contract literally needs revision. Mitigation in keyJudgment: a C-2 ADR redeclaring ベース as a derived measurement with a layout-tuning target band, before PRD-004 commits.

evidence.observation: Skill §3 (Culture & parlor atmosphere) identifies the 常連 (regular) player archetype as 'know every machine's spec, track ハマり, optimize seating. Want depth and accurate math.' This player archetype is precisely what nail-adjustment serves — depth, machine personality, optimization.
evidence.notes: Evidence against H2 (drift): the chosen direction serves the player archetype the project's own canon-grounded analysis identifies as most valuable. Adding depth where regulars want depth is C-9-SPIRIT-aligned even if C-9-LETTER-stretched.

evidence.observation: Iter-3 PRD-003 R-44 explicitly normalizes text rendering to ≤6 distinct sizes ({14, 18, 22, 32, 48, 96}) to quiet macroquad's font-atlas churn warnings. The iter-3 build did not enforce this — `cargo test` does not check rule R-44; the lint is human-driven. Multiple sizes (14, 16, 18, 20, 22, 24, 28, 32, ...) appear in render.rs.
evidence.notes: Mild cherrypick evidence — an unenforced PRD rule that was nominally written but never followed through. Iter-4 must NOT add R-N rules that won't be enforced; either add a test/lint or don't make the rule.
